messageFlowGroups.label: Conversation
messageFlowGroups.description: Complete conversation lifecycle: invite -> accept/decline -> walk -> chat -> end. NPCs auto-accept invites. Messages trigger NPC replies via Claude.

messageFlowGroups.label: Movement
messageFlowGroups.description: Two movement systems: WASD keys (input_start/stop -> velocity + collision) and click-to-move (move -> A* pathfinding). Both produce player_update broadcasts.

messageFlowGroups.label: Lifecycle
messageFlowGroups.description: Player connection and disconnection. Join spawns a player at a map spawn point. Disconnect cleans up conversations and removes the player.

stateMachines.label: Conversation Lifecycle
stateMachines.description: Governs the lifecycle of a conversation between two players. NPCs auto-accept invites (no client UI). Walking state navigates both players toward a rendezvous point. Active state allows message exchange until timeout, max messages, or manual end.

stateMachines.label: Player State
stateMachines.description: Governs what a player is currently doing. Movement and conversations are mutually exclusive: a conversing player cannot move, and starting movement cancels any pending path. The 'doing_activity' state is for future activity interactions.

transitions.condition: Future: player interacts with map activity
Future: player finishes or leaves activity

boundaries.label: Browser Client
boundaries.description: Rendering, prediction, DOM UI, and browser-side debugging.

boundaries.label: Game Server
boundaries.description: Authoritative simulation, transport bridge, debug surfaces, NPC orchestration, and optional Postgres persistence.

cards.title: main.ts
cards.subtitle: browser bootstrap
cards.summary: Owns client bootstrap state, wires renderer/prediction/UI together, and mediates the main browser-side message loop.

openNext.label: main.ts
openNext.reason: Entry point for browser state ownership and wiring.

openNext.label: renderer.ts
openNext.reason: Shows how visual state is projected onto Pixi.

openNext.label: prediction.ts
openNext.reason: Explains client/server movement parity and reconciliation.

cards.title: renderer
cards.subtitle: PixiJS scene graph
cards.summary: Owns the Pixi scene graph and turns synchronized game state into tiles, activity markers, player sprites, and chat bubbles.

openNext.label: renderer.ts
openNext.reason: Shows how clicks and synchronized state become visuals.

openNext.label: main.ts
openNext.reason: Caller that feeds state and inputs into the renderer.

openNext.label: prediction.ts
openNext.reason: Pairs with renderer when reconciling movement.

cards.title: prediction
cards.subtitle: local physics mirror
cards.summary: Mirrors movement physics in the browser so held-key input feels immediate before the authoritative server update arrives.

openNext.label: prediction.ts
openNext.reason: Defines the local movement mirror and reconciliation.

openNext.label: main.ts
openNext.reason: Owns the held-input state that drives prediction.

openNext.label: gameLoop.ts
openNext.reason: Authoritative movement rules that prediction mirrors.

cards.title: ui
cards.subtitle: DOM sidebar
cards.summary: Owns the DOM sidebar surfaces for chat, player list, and conversation controls, then translates user actions into client messages.

openNext.label: ui.ts
openNext.reason: Maps DOM controls to game-facing actions.

openNext.label: main.ts
openNext.reason: Passes state and callbacks into the UI surface.

openNext.label: network.ts
openNext.reason: Contains the browser WebSocket client used by UI actions.

cards.title: debugLog
cards.subtitle: client ring buffer
cards.summary: Keeps a small browser-side event buffer and exposes debug helpers so local client behavior can be inspected without touching the server.

openNext.label: debugLog.ts
openNext.reason: Shows the browser-side inspection surface.

openNext.label: main.ts
openNext.reason: One of the main producers/consumers of client debug events.

cards.title: GameWebSocketServer
cards.subtitle: server/src/network/websocket.ts
cards.summary: Accepts browser sockets, routes client protocol messages into engine mutations, and fans authoritative game events back out as public server messages.

openNext.label: websocket.ts
openNext.reason: Shows protocol ingress, event fanout, and player scoping.

openNext.label: protocol.ts
openNext.reason: Defines the discriminated unions this server routes.

openNext.label: gameLoop.ts
openNext.reason: Consumes the commands and direct calls coming from the socket bridge.

cards.title: Debug API
cards.subtitle: Express router
cards.summary: Exposes read-only state inspection plus a small set of queued and direct debug mutations for local harnesses and scenario control.

openNext.label: router.ts
openNext.reason: Defines every debug API route and whether it is read-only, queued, or direct.

openNext.label: scenarios.ts
openNext.reason: Contains the named runtime presets loaded by /scenario.

openNext.label: gameLoop.ts
openNext.reason: Shows the authoritative state these routes inspect or mutate.

cards.title: ENGINE
cards.subtitle: I/O-free simulation core
cards.summary: Owns the authoritative tick-based state machine for movement, collisions, pathing, conversations, and event emission without any network or database dependencies.

childCards.title: World
childCards.subtitle: world.ts
childCards.summary: Owns the map grid, activity fixtures, and spawn points.

openNext.label: gameLoop.ts
openNext.reason: Central authority for runtime state, commands, and tick processing.

openNext.label: conversation.ts
openNext.reason: Conversation lifecycle and its state transitions live here.

openNext.label: world.ts
openNext.reason: Defines the map, activities, and spawn surfaces the engine owns.

cards.title: NPC STACK
cards.subtitle: server/src/npc/*
cards.summary: Listens to engine lifecycle events, schedules NPC replies and initiations, retrieves relevant memories, and coordinates persistence-facing memory writes.

childCards.title: NpcOrchestrator
childCards.subtitle: orchestrator.ts
childCards.summary: Subscribes to game events, schedules replies, and triggers initiations/reflections.

childCards.title: MemoryManager
childCards.subtitle: memory.ts
childCards.summary: Scores, summarizes, and writes memory records used by NPC prompting.

childCards.title: Provider Stack
childCards.subtitle: provider.ts / resilientProvider.ts
childCards.summary: Composes the primary model provider with scripted fallback behavior.

openNext.label: orchestrator.ts
openNext.reason: Coordinates event subscriptions, reply timing, and initiative.

openNext.label: memory.ts
openNext.reason: Explains retrieval, scoring, summarization, and reflection logic.

openNext.label: resilientProvider.ts
openNext.reason: Shows how model fallback behavior is enforced.

cards.title: PERSISTENCE
cards.subtitle: server/src/db/*
cards.summary: Provides durable storage contracts for memories, NPC state, conversations, and generated artifacts, with in-memory fallback when Postgres is unavailable.

childCards.title: MemoryStore
childCards.subtitle: repository.ts
childCards.summary: Memory repository interface with Postgres and in-memory implementations.

childCards.title: NpcPersistenceStore
childCards.subtitle: npcStore.ts
childCards.summary: NPC persistence interface with durable and fallback implementations.

childCards.title: PostgreSQL + pgvector
childCards.subtitle: schema.sql
childCards.summary: Schema contains relational tables plus pgvector indexing for memory search.

openNext.label: repository.ts
openNext.reason: Defines the memory repository interface and fallback behavior.

openNext.label: npcStore.ts
openNext.reason: Defines how NPC state is stored and restored.

openNext.label: schema.sql
openNext.reason: Shows the physical tables and vector index used by persistence.
